IN RULING ON THIS MATTER, THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE THIRD PARTY CLAIM OF TINA WU. BAY CITY VIEW, LLC HAS A VALID CLAIM AND A SUPERIOR RIGHT OVER THE CHAN FAMILY TRUST TO THE RENTS OF THE TRUST PROPERTY. THERE HAS BEEN NO PRIOR ORDER THAT THE TRUST PROPERTY COULD NOT BE ACCESSED TO SATISFY THE JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Notice Of Hearing On Third-Party Claim Of Jenny Chan, A Co-Trustee Of The Chan Family Trust
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DEBORAH J. WILSON, ESQ. of McInerney & Dillon PC, Tel No. 510-465-7100 appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. ROGER R. RUBIN, ESQ., Tel No. 415-441-1112 appearing for Third Party Claimant Chan Family Trust.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to the hearing On Third-Party Claim Of Jenny Chan, A Co-Trustee Of The Chan Family Trust, the third party claim of Jenny Chan, a co-trustee of the Chan Family Trust, is denied. In ruling on this matter, the court did not consider the third party claim of Tina Wu. Bay City View, LLC has a valid claim and a superior right over the Chan Family Trust to the rents of the trust property. The Chan family Trust is a revocable trust and Derrick and Jenny Chan are the settlors of the trust. (Bay City View, LLC Ex. 1.) Under Probate Code section 18200, the trust property is subject to the claims of creditors of the settlors. (Probate Code section 18200; Keitel v. Heubel (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 324, 330, 337.) There has been no prior order that the trust property could not be accessed to satisfy the judgment. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
